highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.176 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds multilingual session titles that automatically match the language of your conversation, with an optional 'language' setting to lock it to a specific language. It also introduces 'footerLinksRegexes' for adding custom regex-matched link badges in the footer, useful for teams managing multiple projects. Bedrock credential handling has also been improved.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.175 Released
highlights.summary: The new 'enforceAvailableModels' managed setting lets admins lock down which models users can access, ensuring that even the Default model stays within an approved allowlist. If the Default would resolve to a disallowed model, it now automatically falls back to the first allowed model, preventing unexpected model usage. This is especially useful for enterprise or team deployments where cost or compliance controls matter.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.174 Released
highlights.summary: This update fixes the '/model' picker so that Opus now appears as its own selectable row for Max, Team Premium, and Enterprise plan users, making it easier to switch models intentionally. A new 'wheelScrollAccelerationEnabled' setting lets you disable mouse-wheel scroll acceleration in fullscreen mode for a smoother browsing experience. These are small but impactful quality-of-life improvements for daily users.

highlights.title: Show HN: macOS Menu Bar Gauges for Your Claude Code Quota
highlights.summary: This open-source tool adds real-time Claude Code quota usage gauges directly to your macOS menu bar, so you can monitor your API limits without leaving your editor. It's a handy way to avoid hitting quota walls mid-session, especially if you're on a capped plan. Check the GitHub repo to install and configure it for your account.

highlights.title: Running Claude Code Offline on an M3 Pro with Qwen3.6
highlights.summary: This guide walks through running Claude Code in a fully air-gapped environment using a local Qwen3.6 model on an Apple M3 Pro, making it viable for secure or offline development setups. It's particularly relevant for SREs and developers in regulated industries who cannot send code to external APIs. The step-by-step handbook format makes it approachable even if you haven't run local models before.

highlights.title: Statement on the US Government Directive to Suspend Access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
highlights.summary: Anthropic has published an official statement explaining that access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models has been suspended following a US government export control directive. Developers currently using these models in Claude Code should check which model they are defaulting to, as automatic fallback behavior may now apply (see v2.1.175 release notes). This is an important heads-up for teams relying on these specific models in production workflows.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises a fast-paced walkthrough of Claude Code from the basics all the way to more advanced usage patterns, all within 20 minutes. It's a great starting point if you want a single video that covers the full workflow without committing to a long course. Watch it alongside the official docs to reinforce what you learn.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This video aims to give developers a thorough grounding in Claude Code within a focused 30-minute session, likely covering key workflows, shortcuts, and productivity patterns. It's well-suited for developers who already know the basics and want to level up their efficiency. Pair it with the latest release notes to make sure you're also using the newest features.

tips_of_the_day: If you're on a capped Claude Code plan, install a quota monitor (like the macOS menu bar tool from today's digest) so you can see your remaining usage at a glance and avoid mid-session interruptions.

After any Claude Code update, quickly scan the release notes for new 'managed settings' — features like 'enforceAvailableModels' can silently change which model your team defaults to, so it's worth knowing before it surprises you in production.

If your workflow involves sensitive code that can't leave your machine, explore running Claude Code with a local model in air-gapped mode — tools like Qwen3.6 on Apple Silicon now make this practical without sacrificing too much capability.
